description: ol-undefined-var (issue #113): a `for` binder lives only within its own loop body, shadowing an outer binding of the same name and never leaking past the end of that body (spec/execution-model.md:317-327). Reading `:i` inside the loop (line 2) is fine, but reading `:i` again after `end` (line 4) is a read with no visible declaration in scope, so it raises `ol-undefined-var`. This locks in the fix for a genuine scoping bug in the rule's first draft, which treated every declaration as a whole-program-visible flat name.
